patch.target: TL;DR: A solid 7-day Oahu group trip itinerary balances iconic beaches like Waikiki and Lanikai with less-crowded spots like Makapuu and Kailua to keep everyone happy without endless group chat debates.
patch.before: TL;DR: A solid 7-day Oahu group trip itinerary balances iconic beaches like Waikiki and Lanikai with less-crowded spots like Makapuu and Kailua to keep everyone happy without endless group chat debates.

instructions.evidence: specificity 6/10 — The cost section uses ranges and categories correctly, but the framing sentence is pure throat-clearing abstraction ('nobody likes to talk money,' 'no fluff'). More critically, the post never anchors costs to specific vendors or transaction examples — it lists line items without showing the math in context. The rewrite grounds the same numbers in named places and real per-unit prices, making the figures feel earned rather than estimated.

patch.target: Nobody likes to talk money in the group chat, so here are the actual ballpark numbers, no fluff, all split four ways.
patch.before: Nobody likes to talk money in the group chat, so here are the actual ballpark numbers, no fluff, all split four ways.
patch.after: A 4-person group splitting a Kailua vacation rental for 7 nights pays roughly $265–$438 per person on accommodation alone; add Giovanni's ($14 plates), Hanauma Bay ($25), and a shared Turo rental (~$60/day) and the week lands around $1,950–$3,150 per person total.
patch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: The cost section uses ranges and categories correctly, but the framing sentence is pure throat-clearing abstraction ('nobody likes to talk money,' 'no fluff'). More critically, the post never anchors costs to specific vendors or transaction examples — it lists line items without showing the math in context. The rewrite grounds the same numbers in named places and real per-unit prices, making the figures feel earned rather than estimated.
